notes: These files are designed for rendering detailed type information, not just range checking.
Fields are intentionally verbose so the UI can choose what to show.
mainstreamSizes describes common ABIs/platforms; standardGuarantee describes what the language standard itself promises.
languageNotes: Java primitive numeric types have fixed sizes and two's-complement / IEEE semantics specified by the language.
Unlike C/C++, Java does not vary primitive widths by ABI or platform data model.

types.summary: Smallest ordinary integer primitive. Good for raw bytes, compact arrays, protocol fields, and I/O buffers, but all arithmetic promotes to int.
types.standardGuarantee: Exactly 8 bits, range -128..127, same on every Java implementation.
specialRules: Arithmetic on byte promotes to int before the operation result type is chosen.
Narrowing conversions back to byte may lose high bits.
Overflow in byte arithmetic occurs after promotion/truncation steps and is well-defined according to two's-complement narrowing behavior.

types.summary: Larger than byte but still narrower than int. Used in compact storage and some binary formats; arithmetic still promotes to int.
types.standardGuarantee: Exactly 16 bits, range -32768..32767 on every Java implementation.
specialRules: Arithmetic promotes to int.
Narrowing casts may discard high bits.

types.kind: unsigned integral primitive / UTF-16 code unit
types.definition: 16-bit unsigned primitive representing UTF-16 code units.
types.summary: Java char is not a full Unicode code point type; it stores one UTF-16 code unit. Supplementary characters need surrogate pairs.
specialRules: A single Java char may represent only half of a surrogate pair.
Arithmetic involving char usually promotes to int.
Do not equate char count with Unicode user-perceived character count.

types.summary: Default integer type for most Java code and the target of arithmetic promotion for smaller integral types.
types.standardGuarantee: Exactly 32 bits, range -2^31..2^31-1 on every JVM.
specialRules: Overflow wraps according to two's-complement arithmetic; Java does not make signed overflow undefined.
Division by zero on integer types throws ArithmeticException.
Use Integer.compareUnsigned or dedicated APIs when you want unsigned interpretation tricks.

types.summary: Primary large fixed-width integer primitive for ordinary Java code. Suffix L/l used in literals.
types.standardGuarantee: Exactly 64 bits, range -2^63..2^63-1 on every JVM.
specialRules: Overflow wraps in two's-complement sense.
Use BigInteger for arbitrary precision integer arithmetic.

types.summary: Single-precision float. Less precision than double; literals need suffix F/f unless assignment context narrows a constant expression legally.
types.standardGuarantee: Exactly IEEE 754 binary32 in modern Java specification wording.
types.precision: 24 bits of precision including the hidden bit; about 6-7 decimal digits.
types.rounding: IEEE 754 rounding semantics; Java now specifies strict floating-point behavior more tightly than very old historical folklore suggests.
specialRules: 0.1f is not exact.
NaN is unordered and not equal to itself.
Use Float.floatToRawIntBits for bit-level inspection.

types.summary: Default floating type in Java. Better precision/range than float; still not exact for most decimal fractions.
types.standardGuarantee: Exactly IEEE 754 binary64 in modern Java specification wording.
types.precision: 53 bits of precision; about 15-16 decimal digits.
specialRules: All integers up to 2^53 are exactly representable.
NaN != NaN.
Signed zero matters.

types.definition: Immutable arbitrary-precision signed integer class from java.math.
types.summary: Use when fixed-width overflow is unacceptable or when the problem domain is mathematically integer-valued without practical bit limits.
types.standardGuarantee: Library type, not primitive. Precision grows as needed subject to memory and implementation limits.
specialRules: Arithmetic allocates new objects.
No fixed machine width.
Best for exact large integer math, not hot-loop primitive arithmetic.

types.definition: Immutable arbitrary-precision decimal class with explicit scale and configurable rounding.
types.summary: Best fit for exact decimal business rules, money calculations with explicit rounding policies, and decimal strings that must not suffer binary floating approximations.
types.standardGuarantee: Library type, not primitive. Decimal precision/scale semantics are defined by the class API rather than primitive width.
specialRules: Construct from string for exact human-decimal intent.
Division may require explicit rounding mode.
Equality checks include scale unless using compareTo.
